prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark, indistinct image. The content is not clearly visible in the first sampled frame, apart from a dim rectangular area against a dark background.

By around 24 seconds, the image has changed to a product-and-text display. On the left is a tall red/pink product package labeled “today’s girl,” with a photo or illustration of a seated woman on the lower part of the box. Near the upper center/right is a square image of a smiling blonde woman’s face. To the right and below the face image, large white text reads:

“Choose it for  
the quality.  
Buy it for  
the price.  
$1.19”

The final frame keeps the same layout, now clearer against a mostly black background: the “today’s girl” package remains on the left, the smiling blonde woman’s headshot is above/right, and the white promotional text and price remain centered on the right. No other actions or people are visible in the sampled frames.
